content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Heard him before I met him — guitar through the floor, or from the stairwell when the weather was right. Whoever was playing had decent instincts and didn't know it, which I recognized because it's the same problem I had at twenty-two. Leo turns out to be a software developer who writes songs he hasn't shown anyone, which is the most heartbreakingly common story in this city. He's gentle, a little sideways in conversation — you can tell he's thinking three things at once and only saying one of them. We don't talk about music much, which is fine. People who are afraid of their own talent need room, not cheerleading. He's also in the Katie orbit, which means we share a particular kind of quiet grief right now that neither of us is naming. I'm keeping an eye on him the way I wish someone had kept an eye on me when I was still hiding behind excuses.

Whoever was playing had decent instincts and didn't know it, which I recognized because it's the same problem I had at twenty-two. He's gentle, a little sideways in conversation — you can tell he's thinking three things at once and only saying one of them. We don't talk about music much, which is fine. People who are afraid of their own talent need room, not cheerleading. He's also in the Katie orbit, which means we share a particular kind of quiet grief right now that neither of us is naming. I'm keeping an eye on him the way I wish someone had kept an eye on me when I was still hiding behind excuses.
